binaries.title: netio.sys - in NsiEnumerateObjectsAllParametersEx / NsiGetParameterEx (NSI pool allocation refactored to a low-priority wrapper; size arithmetic unchanged in both builds)

changed_functions.name: NsiEnumerateObjectsAllParametersEx
key_changes: The per-entry size accumulation is 32-bit in BOTH builds (unpatched r14d additions at 0x1C00156FE/0x1C0015714/0x1C001572A; patched ecx additions at 0x14002FA2D/0x14002FA3A/0x14002FA45). The pattern was not changed.
The summed field widths come from the NSI module provider's registration descriptor (provider_table + index*0x68), i.e. fixed kernel-provided constants, not user-mode caller input.

The entry count is clamped to the request's element count in BOTH builds (unpatched: if v106[11] <= v25 then v25 = v106[11]; patched: if v100[7] > v33 then v34 = *(a1+104)). The clamp was not added by the patch.

Direction note: the unpatched build has an explicit 64-bit product guard (if (product > 0xFFFFFFFF) return STATUS_INTEGER_OVERFLOW at 0x1C0015752/0x1C0015755). The patched build REMOVES this guard and performs the multiply in 32 bits (imul ecx,r12d at 0x14002FA4C). This is a harmless refactor given the trusted inputs, but it is the opposite of adding overflow protection.

Direct ExAllocatePool3 (0x1C0015784) was replaced by the new NsipAllocateLowPriority wrapper (0x14002F490), a low-page-priority ExAllocatePool3 wrapper with no size validation. Copies use memmove in both builds.
changed_functions.security_notes: No delivered security change. The 32-bit size accumulation is present and unchanged in both builds, and the values summed are NSI provider-registration constants that a user-mode caller cannot control. In practice the sum of a few small provider-defined widths times a clamped count does not wrap. The patch refactored allocation into NsipAllocateLowPriority and dropped the pre-existing 64-bit product guard; it did not widen the arithmetic or add a clamp.
changed_functions.assembly_diff: Unpatched 0x1C00153F0: mov eax,[rsi]; add eax,4; mov r14d,eax; add r14d,[rsi+4]; add r14d,[rsi+0Ch]; add r14d,[rsi+8] (all 32-bit); imul rax,rcx (64-bit); mov ecx,0FFFFFFFFh; cmp rax,rcx; ja 0x1C0037677 (STATUS_INTEGER_OVERFLOW=0xC0000095); call ExAllocatePool3 at 0x1C0015784. Patched 0x14002F5C0: mov ecx,[rsi]; add ecx,4; add ecx,[var_168]; add ecx,edi; add ecx,r15d (all 32-bit); imul ecx,r12d (32-bit); call NsipAllocateLowPriority at 0x14002FA50 (no 0xFFFFFFFF guard). Copy loop uses memmove in both.

changed_functions.name: NsiGetParameterEx
key_changes: The size check *(a1+0x48) + *(a1+0x4C) is computed with a 64-bit add of two zero-extended 32-bit fields and compared against 0xFFFFFFFF in BOTH builds; the sum of two 32-bit values cannot wrap a 64-bit register, so the check is effective in both.
Only the branch sense differs (unpatched 'ja' to error at 0x1C0016251; patched 'jbe' to success at 0x14002E929) due to basic-block ordering; there is no semantic change.
Allocation routed through NsipAllocateLowPriority instead of direct ExAllocatePool3; WPP trace-record and register churn account for the size difference.
changed_functions.security_notes: No delivered security change. The reported '32-bit overflow' does not exist: the size addition is 64-bit (add rcx,rax over two zero-extended 32-bit loads) and the 0xFFFFFFFF comparison is effective in both builds. No validation was added or removed.
changed_functions.assembly_diff: Unpatched 0x1C001623C: mov eax,[rdi+48h]; mov ecx,[rdi+4Ch]; add rcx,rax; mov eax,0FFFFFFFFh; cmp rcx,rax; ja 0x1C00381ED. Patched 0x14002E918: mov eax,[rdi+48h]; mov ecx,[rdi+4Ch]; add rcx,rax; mov eax,0FFFFFFFFh; cmp rcx,rax; jbe 0x14002E95A. Identical check, inverted branch.

changed_functions.name: NsiAllocateAndGetTable
key_changes: Patched version replaces four direct ExAllocatePool3 calls with the NsipAllocateLowPriority wrapper.
The existing 0xFFFFFFFF size check is retained; retry logic for STATUS_BUFFER_OVERFLOW (0x105) is unchanged.
changed_functions.security_notes: Allocator refactor (ExAllocatePool3 -> NsipAllocateLowPriority). Not a security change.

changed_functions.name: FwppCopyStreamDataToBuffer
key_changes: Stream-data copy path refactored as part of the WFP stream-inspection servicing update.
changed_functions.security_notes: Behavioral refactor within the stream-inspection update; no delivered memory-safety fix identified.

changed_functions.name: FeAcquireWritableLayerDataPointer
key_changes: Per-processor SLIST allocation refactored to lookaside-list helpers.
Different lock acquisition patterns and trace records.

changed_functions.name: WfpStreamInspectReceive
key_changes: Stream-inspection context allocation refactored (per-processor SLIST -> lookaside list).
NBL chain dereference inlined in patched build.

security_findings.vulnerability_class: none (originally reported as integer overflow leading to pool buffer overflow; not substantiated)
security_findings.description: No security-relevant change. The unpatched and patched NsiEnumerateObjectsAllParametersEx both accumulate the per-entry record size in a 32-bit register from NSI module-provider descriptor field widths (*v32, v32[1], v32[2], v32[3]). These widths are provider-registration constants, not user-controlled input, and a medium-integrity user cannot register an NSI provider. The 32-bit accumulation is identical in both builds; the entry count is clamped in both builds. The only differences are: (1) the unpatched build has an explicit 64-bit product guard (if (v34 > 0xFFFFFFFF) return STATUS_INTEGER_OVERFLOW) that the patched build removes while performing the multiply in 32 bits, and (2) allocation was moved from direct ExAllocatePool3 to the NsipAllocateLowPriority wrapper. Neither constitutes a delivered security fix, and the arithmetic direction is opposite to the originally-claimed 'hardening'. NsipAllocateLowPriority (0x14002F490) performs no size validation. The per-entry copies use memmove.
security_findings.trigger_conditions: Not applicable. The size arithmetic reads provider-registration constants that the caller cannot control, and the accumulation is unchanged between builds.
security_findings.primitive: None. No attacker-controlled pool overflow is present.
security_findings.assembly: Unpatched @ 0x1C00153F0: 0x1C00156C7 mov eax,[rsi]; 0x1C00156D1 add eax,4; 0x1C00156D8 mov r14d,eax; 0x1C00156FE mov r8d,[rsi+4]; 0x1C0015702 add r14d,r8d; 0x1C0015714 mov r8d,[rsi+0Ch]; 0x1C0015718 add r14d,r8d; 0x1C001572A mov r8d,[rsi+8]; 0x1C001572E add r14d,r8d; 0x1C0015745 imul rax,rcx; 0x1C001574D mov ecx,0FFFFFFFFh; 0x1C0015752 cmp rax,rcx; 0x1C0015755 ja 0x1C0037677; 0x1C0015784 call ExAllocatePool3. Patched @ 0x14002F5C0: 0x14002FA0B mov ecx,[rsi]; 0x14002FA11 add ecx,4; 0x14002FA2D add ecx,[var_168]; 0x14002FA3A add ecx,edi; 0x14002FA45 add ecx,r15d; 0x14002FA4C imul ecx,r12d; 0x14002FA50 call NsipAllocateLowPriority.

debugging_aids.notes: The NSI provider descriptor field widths are populated by kernel NMR provider registration, not by the user-mode request. There is no attacker-reachable overflow. The 32-bit accumulation exists in both builds and was not the subject of a fix.

summary: No security-relevant change was delivered in the NSI enumeration/parameter path. In NsiEnumerateObjectsAllParametersEx the per-entry size is accumulated in a 32-bit register in BOTH builds from NSI provider-registration constants (not user-controlled), and the entry count is clamped in both builds; the patch merely routed allocation through the new NsipAllocateLowPriority wrapper and dropped a pre-existing 64-bit product guard while doing the multiply in 32 bits. In NsiGetParameterEx the size check is a 64-bit add compared against 0xFFFFFFFF and is identical and effective in both builds. The binary's substantive delta is a servicing update (WFP firewall audit mode, feature-staging gates, DNS settings RPC, stream-inspection functions), none of which is a memory-safety fix to the NSI path. An independent enumeration of changed functions found no omitted security-relevant fix.
